name: note-c architecture map
purpose: Portable C SDK for communicating with a Blues Notecard over serial or I2C.
principles: Put platform-specific behavior behind hooks or adapter libraries.
Treat note.h and hook signatures as compatibility contracts.
Update the Markdown, HTML, and JSON maps when architecture-relevant code changes land.

layers.name: Consumers and adapters
layers.role: Applications, platform SDKs, and tests that call note-c.

layers.name: Public contract
layers.role: The API and hook surface downstream code depends on.

layers.name: Portable core
layers.role: Request lifecycle, JSON, helpers, binary codecs, and utility behavior.

layers.name: Transport and hooks
layers.role: Transport selection, chunking, and platform callback boundaries.

layers.name: Hardware and cloud boundary
layers.role: Physical bus, Notecard, and Notehub reached by the Notecard.

nodes.name: Applications
nodes.summary: User firmware or host apps that build requests and consume responses.

nodes.name: Adapter SDKs
nodes.summary: Platform integrations such as note-arduino, note-zephyr, note-espidf, and POSIX integrations.
contracts: Own platform setup and hook implementations

nodes.name: Unit Tests
nodes.summary: Mock-backed tests for JSON, request flow, hooks, transports, helpers, and edge cases.

nodes.name: note.h Public API
nodes.summary: Public functions, typedefs, constants, version metadata, request helpers, and hook signatures.
contracts: Breaking changes require versioning and migration notes

nodes.name: Request Core
nodes.summary: Creates, serializes, sends, retries, parses, and releases Notecard request/response transactions.

nodes.name: JSON Model
nodes.summary: Bundled JSON object model and helper APIs used by callers and internals.

nodes.name: High-level Helpers
nodes.summary: Convenience APIs for common Notecard operations: time, env, location, sync, payload, binary store, and status helpers.
contracts: Builds JSON requests on top of public request APIs

nodes.name: Portable Utilities
nodes.summary: String, number, formatting, encoding, hashing, constants, and user-agent support.

nodes.name: Platform Hooks
nodes.summary: Registration and invocation of memory, time, mutex, debug, transaction, serial, I2C, and JSON transaction callbacks.

nodes.name: Serial Transport
nodes.summary: Serial transaction, reset, receive, transmit, and chunking behavior.

nodes.name: I2C Transport
nodes.summary: I2C transaction, reset, query length, receive, transmit, MTU, and chunking behavior.
contracts: Owns I2C timing and chunking behavior

nodes.name: Platform Bus
nodes.summary: UART, USB serial, I2C, or test doubles supplied by the host platform.

nodes.name: Notecard
nodes.summary: Device that accepts JSON requests and returns JSON responses.

nodes.name: Notehub
nodes.summary: Cloud service reached by the Notecard; note-c does not communicate with Notehub directly.

hotspots.title: Public API and hook compatibility
hotspots.description: The stable SDK surface that applications and adapter libraries compile against, including public request helpers and platform hook signatures.
hotspots.updateRule: Update docs and consider versioning whenever note.h contracts change.

hotspots.title: Request lifecycle
hotspots.description: The path a Notecard transaction follows as requests are built, serialized, sent over an active transport, parsed, and returned to callers.
hotspots.updateRule: Update docs when timeout, retry, serialization, parsing, memory ownership, or active-interface behavior changes.

hotspots.title: Transport and platform boundary
hotspots.description: The byte-level boundary between portable note-c transport code and host-provided UART, USB serial, I2C, or test-double implementations.
hotspots.updateRule: Update docs when byte framing, chunking, MTU, reset, or hook invocation semantics change.

hotspots.title: Adapter impact
hotspots.description: The parts of note-c most likely to require coordinated changes in downstream platform SDKs and integration layers.
hotspots.updateRule: Update docs when note-arduino, note-zephyr, note-espidf, POSIX, or other adapters need coordinated changes.

updateTriggers: Changes to note.h public APIs, typedefs, constants, or hook signatures.
Changes to request lifecycle, timeout, retry, memory ownership, or response handling.
Changes to serial/I2C framing, chunking, transmit, receive, reset, or MTU behavior.
Changes to JSON representation or helper semantics.
Changes to adapter expectations, build/test strategy, release strategy, or versioning.
filesToUpdateTogether: docs/architecture/decisions/*.md when a durable design decision changes
